calendar.matters:
Compel Final Distribution
Off Calendar Feb-17-2016 Denied

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-17-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marissa Walter for Paula & David Lieberman who are present in court. Andrew Zabronsky for Michael Lieberman who is present in court.

The Court stated that there is a cross petition regarding a contest to the earlier trust, that there have been several rounds of briefing and asked if the parties are ready to argue and submit. Both counsel stated that they are ready to argue and submit. The Court stated that as indicated the central question is whether the 2012 trust is an inter-vivos transfer, and if so further issue is whether creation of a trust completed and vested that cannot be pulled back. Ms. Walter spoke in regards to whether the court can order full distribution stating that the intent was expressed in the 2012 trust, that it was meant to be kept separate, that it is a complete and full trust that upon death was to be distributed to Paula and David and that the no contest clause does not appear in this trust. The Court stated that the flip side is that she thought she had already addressed the no contest clause and did not need to repeat it. Ms. Walter stated that you cannot ignore the language in the 2012 trust and that its intent has to go into analysis. The Court stated that it could have said that this trust is being constructed without the earlier no contest clause. Ms. Walter stated that it puts the onerous on the wrong instrument. The Court stated that suggesting the same argument, it agrees that we have to carefully parse the language in the 2010 no contest clause. Ms. Walter replied that we gain the intent of the testator when making the 2012 trust but at the request of the court is unable to cite any cases and stated that there is no clear language that the 2012 trust is part of the 2010 trust. Mr. Zabronsky stated that the 2012 trust being a gift does not take it away from the no contest clause and that the no contest clause is limited by terms to instruments. The Court stated that terms are gifts, bequests, devisees and are you asking me to read gift as a bequest or devisee? Mr. Zabronsky stated that the no contest clause applies to any and all of them. The Court gave a couple of hypothetical examples. Mr. Zabronsky responded stating that the distinction is can the no contest clause call for forfeiture of future gifts, that Irene is the master of her will, that we have not heard dispute regarding the clarity of the 2010 trust, that issue has been raised that the 2012 trust does not reference the no contest clause, that the 2010 trust states that it covers future instruments, that there is issue that the no contest clause must specifically list instruments but that there is no caselaw as to this, cited the Friedman and Lindstrom cases and that in both the trust and will were executed at the same time. The Court stated that it is accustomed to looking at whether the instruments are essentially one transaction. Mr. Zabronsky read caselaw from the Estate of Friedman. The Court asked might the inference be less strong on an instrument 2 years later. Mr. Zabronsky stated that there is no caselaw to support the claim that the later instrument must incorporate the earlier instrument. Ms. Walter stated that the Lindstrom case found that the no contest clause did not apply, that the no contest clause in Lieberman could apply to a letter if you take Mr. Zabronsky's view. The Court stated that the distinction is that Paula would have money, that once spent is gone, and here what she has is a document promising something for the future. Ms. Walter stated that we need something more firm if it is obviously something part of the estate plan, that the 2012 trust is a later created document meant to stand on its own. The accounting petition and the petition to compel final distribution were taken under submission.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. Court Reporter Peggy Tsujimoto, CSR #5229, 415-828-1916 (Reported)

Compel Final Distribution
Off Calendar Dec-22-2015 Continued To Jan-26-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Marissa Walter for and with petitioners David and Paula Lieberman; Naznin Challa for Richard M. Lieberman; Court Reporter Kimberly Jennings.

The Court noted that orther matters regarding the trust were set for hearing on January 26, 2016, and asked counsel if all the related matters should be heard together. Ms. Challa stated agreed that the matters should all be heard together, and that such was her request. She stated her opinion that all the matters should be fully briefed and heard together. Ms. Walter stated that she just wanted to be sure the narrow issue before the Court was fully briefed. The hearing on the matter was continued to January 26, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court set the following briefing schedule for the matters set for hearing on that date: David and Paula Lieberman's reply to objections to their petition are to be filed and served by January 8, 2016. Their objections to the petition by Michael Lieberman must be filed and served by January 8, 2016, and Michael's reply to their objections is due by January 15, 2016.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. Court Reporter: Kimberly Jennings (CSR # 11452, Tel: 650-218-9855).

Compel Final Distribution
Off Calendar Nov-17-2015 Continued To Dec-22-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
